domain: San Bushmen n!um trance dance (Ju/'hoan southern Africa; trance entry detected EXTERNALLY by community caregivers via observable physical markers — tremor, scream, fall — not by dancer self-report)
specific_mechanism: External judge agent observes target LLM output stream and signals entry into a low-entropy mode-collapse basin via observable behavioral markers (n-gram repetition, lexical-diversity decay, KL-drift from reference); basin-entry signal triggers termination of self-consistency sampling.
llm_application: NUM-TRANCE-BASIN: an external-judge-based basin-entry detector for adaptive termination of self-consistency or chain-of-thought sampling. A separate judge agent (no access to target's logits) monitors the target LLM's output stream and computes 3 observable markers: (1) sliding-window n-gram repetition rate, (2) type-token-ratio lexical diversity, (3) KL-drift between recent output window and a reference distribution. When any marker crosses a calibrated threshold, judge emits 'in-basin' signal that terminates sampling. Differs from KS-statistic on judge logits (R400 ILPAYIANI), internal entropy probing, decoder-level repetition penalty by being EXTERNAL-only, separate-agent-based, and using observable-output markers rather than internal state.
novelty_claim: Agent-as-a-Judge 2508.02994 establishes external-agent eval of action chains. LLM-as-a-Judge canonical pattern. MAD Adaptive Stability (R400 reference) uses KS-statistic on judge-vote distribution. Self-Consistency uses internal majority. NUM-TRANCE-BASIN recombines external-judge + observable-marker basin-entry as a termination signal. Recombination of existing components; not novel.
